paper.title: Long term performance of chloride binding capacity in fly ash concrete in a marine environment
paper.authors: T. Cheewaket; C. Jaturapitakkul; W. Chalee
paper.material_system: Fly ash concrete exposed to marine tidal environment
paper.notes: Packet contains mix proportions and durability/chloride-binding results, but no compressive-strength values.

conditions.exposure_solution: tidal zone seawater in Gulf of Thailand; two wet-dry cycles daily; chloride 16,000-18,000 ppm; sulfate 2200-2600 ppm

conditions.test_method: acid-soluble ASTM C1152 and water-soluble ASTM C1218 chloride tests

evidence.evidence_text: Fly ash concrete with W/B 0.45, 0.55 and 0.65 was exposed in marine tidal zone and chloride binding capacity was studied.
Table 2 lists mixture proportions of concretes in kg/m3 for cement type I, cement type V, fly ash, fine aggregate, coarse aggregate, water and W/B.
Concrete cube specimens of 200 mm were cast and placed into the tidal zone of a marine environment in the Gulf of Thailand for 3, 4, 5 and 7 years.
Core specimens were dry-cut and powdered; acid-soluble chloride conforms to ASTM C1152 and water-soluble chloride conforms to ASTM C1218.

main_risks: No compressive-strength results are present in the supplied packet.
Only mixture and exposure metadata were extracted.
